titleStmt.title: Letter from Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, the Earl of Meath, to Lord Wimborne, 7 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, Lord Lieutenant of County Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A short letter from Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, to Ivor Churchill Guest. Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne (1873-1939) was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

correspAction.persName: Reginald Le Normand Brabazon, 13th Earl of Meath, Lord Lieutenant of County Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Chaworth House, Ottershaw, Chertsey. Feby. 7 1916 My dear Windborne, Thanks for your kind
letter re Reassisting
Committee, which I
am sending to Sir
Maurice Dockrell. I will send you
shortly another name
in the place of that of
Powerscourt. Yours sincerely Meath
